engineIntelligence.h2: Dodge Avenger Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the engines fitted to the Dodge Avenger in the UK - including known failure causes, production years across variants, typical replacement costs, and key technical specs. All prices reflect typical UK market ranges for rebuilt units.

cards.altText: Dodge Avenger BYL engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts flow causing rough idle and power loss on higher-mileage units.
Turbo actuator failure - vacuum-operated wastegate sticks leading to reduced boost and limp mode.

cards.altText: Dodge Avenger EDG engine - petrol 2.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Throttle body failure - electronic throttle control motor fails causing stalling and ETC warning light.
Ignition coil wear - coil packs degrade after 70k miles triggering misfire codes and rough running.

cards.altText: Dodge Avenger ERB engine - petrol 3.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Water pump leak - internal seal failure causes coolant loss and overheating on higher-mileage units.
Cam phaser rattle - VVT actuator wear creates timing noise at startup requiring specialist attention.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £3,000-£4,800 (supply only) Estimated from comparable Pentastar units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Dodge Avenger Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Dodge Avenger across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: VW-sourced 2.0L CRD diesel brought efficient torque to the Avenger range, but EGR and turbo concerns drive UK replacement demand.
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup causes rough idle and power loss after 80k miles
Turbo actuator failure - vacuum wastegate sticks triggering limp mode
Timing chain tensioner wear - documented on higher-mileage BYL units
entries.cta: Get quotes for BYL Dodge Avenger engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level 2.0L World Engine offered affordability but throttle body and ignition coil issues are common replacement drivers.
commonFailures: Throttle body failure - electronic motor fails causing stalling and ETC warning light
Ignition coil wear - misfires and rough running after 70k miles
Thermostat housing crack - plastic housing leaks coolant on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for ECN Dodge Avenger engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Petrol
entries.history: The volume-seller 2.4L World Engine balanced power and economy, though throttle body and coil failures remain common UK concerns.
commonFailures: Throttle body failure - ETC motor degradation causes stalling and warning lights
Ignition coil failure - misfire codes P0300-P0304 after 70k miles
Alternator wear - charging system failure leading to no-start conditions
entries.cta: Get quotes for EDG Dodge Avenger engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L V6 Petrol
entries.history: Mid-range 2.7L V6 offered smoother power but oil sludge and timing concerns limit long-term UK appeal.
commonFailures: Oil sludge buildup - inadequate PCV ventilation causes bearing wear on neglected units
Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling noise at startup requiring specialist attention
Water pump leak - internal seal failure causes coolant loss and overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for EER Dodge Avenger engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L V6 Petrol
entries.history: High-output 3.5L V6 powered the R/T performance trim, with cooling system and head gasket concerns driving rebuild enquiries.
commonFailures: Cooling system failure - radiator and water pump leaks cause overheating on high-mileage units
Head gasket wear - coolant/oil mixing documented on neglected 3.5L units
Valve cover gasket leak - oil seepage onto exhaust creating smoke and odour
entries.cta: Get quotes for EGF Dodge Avenger engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Pentastar V6 Petrol
entries.history: The refined 3.6L Pentastar replaced older V6s in 2011, offering improved efficiency but water pump and cam phaser issues persist.
compatibleVariants: Heat 3.6 Pentastar, R/T 3.6 Pentastar, SXT Plus 3.6 Pentastar
commonFailures: Water pump leak - internal seal failure causes coolant loss and overheating after 80k miles
Cam phaser rattle - VVT actuator wear creates timing noise at cold startup
Oil cooler seal leak - external oil seepage requiring gasket replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERB Dodge Avenger engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Dodge Avenger Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Dodge Avenger's 2.0L diesel (BYL) and 2.4L petrol (EDG) engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with throttle body, ignition coil and cooling system failures most commonly reported. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Avenger.

problems.h4: Throttle Body Failure - Why Dodge Avenger Engines Stall Unexpectedly
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Avenger SE, SXT, Express (2007-2014, ECN & EDG petrol engines)
problems.rootCause: Electronic throttle control motor degrades or carbon buildup restricts butterfly valve movement, triggering limp mode and stalling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove throttle body, clean carbon deposits, reset ECU adaptation values
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may buy 10-20k miles if caught early before motor failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or reconditioned throttle body unit, perform ECU relearn procedure
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable solution; new unit typically lasts 80k+ miles with proper maintenance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt EDG/ECN engine with upgraded throttle components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when engine has other wear; eliminates multiple failure points at once

problems.recommendation: On a Dodge Avenger worth £2,000-£4,000, throttle body replacement (£350-£600 specialist) is usually the sensible first step. If the engine has high mileage or other issues, a rebuilt unit from £1,500 may offer better long-term value with warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EDG engine prices for your Dodge Avenger

problems.h4: Ignition Coil Failure - Why Dodge Avenger Engines Misfire
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Avenger SE, SXT, Express, Mainstreet (2007-2014, ECN & EDG petrol engines)
problems.rootCause: Coil pack insulation breaks down from heat cycling, causing weak spark, misfires (P0300-P0304), and rough running.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty ignition coil and spark plug on affected cylinder
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective if only one coil failed; other coils may follow soon after

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four ignition coils and spark plugs as preventative maintenance
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for engines over 80k miles; reduces repeat failures and labour costs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt EDG/ECN engine with new coils, plugs, and upgraded ignition components
repairOptions.longevity: Best when misfires coincide with other wear; warranty covers future ignition issues

problems.recommendation: For isolated misfires on a well-maintained Avenger, replacing the full coil set (£280-£450) is usually the right call. If the engine burns oil, has timing concerns, or exceeds 120k miles, a rebuilt unit from £1,500 offers peace of mind with a 12-month warranty.
problems.cta: Get quotes for ECN ignition system repair or replacement

problems.h4: Cooling System Failure - Thermostat Housing & Water Pump Issues
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Avenger SE, SXT, R/T, Heat (2007-2014, all petrol engines)
problems.rootCause: Plastic thermostat housing cracks from heat cycling; water pump seals wear, causing coolant loss, overheating, and potential head gasket damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace thermostat, plastic housing, coolant; pressure test system
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught before overheating damage; use upgraded metal housing where available

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace water pump, timing belt/chain components, coolant; full system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended preventative service at 100k miles; addresses two common failure points together

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if overheating caused head gasket or block damage; warranty protects against repeat failure

problems.recommendation: If your Avenger overheated but the engine still runs smoothly, thermostat/water pump replacement (£250-£750) may save the engine. If there's white smoke, coolant in oil, or loss of compression, a rebuilt engine from £1,500 is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt petrol engine prices for your Dodge Avenger

problems.h4: Turbo & EGR Issues - Why Dodge Avenger Diesel Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Avenger SE, SXT 2.0 CRD (2007-2010, BYL diesel engine)
problems.rootCause: EGR valve clogs with carbon; turbo actuator sticks or wastegate fails, causing limp mode, black smoke, and reduced boost pressure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and clean EGR valve, or fit blanking plate; ECU adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may buy 20-30k miles but doesn't address turbo wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo actuator or refurbished turbo core; test boost pressure and EGR function
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable if engine block is sound; refurbished turbo typically lasts 60-80k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt BYL diesel with upgraded turbo, EGR delete option, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when engine has high mileage or other wear; eliminates multiple diesel failure points

problems.recommendation: For limp mode on a BYL Avenger under 100k miles, turbo actuator replacement (£500-£900) is usually worthwhile. If the engine has oil consumption, timing concerns, or exceeds 120k miles, a rebuilt diesel from £1,800 offers better long-term reliability with warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BYL diesel engine replacement UK

problems.h4: Timing Chain Concerns - Preventing Catastrophic Dodge Avenger Engine Failure
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Avenger SE, SXT 2.0 CRD (2007-2010, BYL diesel engine)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wears, allowing chain slack; guides break, causing chain jump, valve timing loss, and potential piston-to-valve contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides; reset timing; oil and filter change
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended at 100k miles or if rattling noise heard; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt BYL diesel with upgraded timing components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if chain jumped or engine suffered interference damage; warranty covers future timing issues

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling at startup on a BYL Avenger, get the timing chain inspected immediately. Preventative replacement (£600-£950) is far cheaper than engine replacement. If the chain has jumped, a rebuilt engine from £1,800 is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt BYL diesel engine prices for your Dodge Avenger

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Dodge Avenger
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Avenger suffers from throttle body issues, ignition coil failures, cooling system leaks, turbo concerns, or timing chain wear, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Dodge Avenger specialists. Every rebuilt and reconditioned engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Dodge Avenger Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Dodge Avenger and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.title: Dodge Avenger Reconditioned Engines (Recon)

types.frontDescription: Professionally stripped, cleaned and rebuilt with new gaskets, seals and worn parts replaced to OEM specification.
types.frontDisclaimer: Prices vary by engine code, variant and supplier.

Supply only or supply & fit available nationwide

items.title: Dodge Avenger 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L VW-sourced CRD diesel (BYL code) was the only diesel option for the UK Avenger, offering strong torque and motorway efficiency but requiring specialist knowledge for EGR and turbo-related repairs.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain tensioner wear at higher mileage

items.title: Dodge Avenger 2.0L Petrol Engines

items.title: Dodge Avenger 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The volume-seller 2.4L GEMA World Engine (EDG code) powered most UK Avengers from 2007-2014, balancing adequate power with reasonable economy, though throttle body and cooling system issues remain prevalent.

items.title: Dodge Avenger 2.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The mid-range 2.7L Chrysler V6 (EER code) offered smoother power for SXT trims in early production years but is less common in the UK due to oil sludge concerns and higher running costs.
commonFailurePoints: Oil sludge buildup from infrequent servicing

items.title: Dodge Avenger 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The high-output 3.5L Chrysler V6 (EGF code) powered the R/T performance trim, delivering strong acceleration but requiring careful cooling system maintenance to avoid head gasket concerns.
commonFailurePoints: head gasket wear on neglected units

items.title: Dodge Avenger 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The refined 3.6L Pentastar V6 (ERB code) replaced older V6s from 2011, offering improved efficiency and power for Heat and late R/T trims, though water pump and cam phaser issues require attention.
commonFailurePoints: cam phaser rattle at cold startup

items.title: Dodge Avenger Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L VW-sourced CRD diesel (BYL code) was the only diesel option for UK Avengers (2007-2010), offering strong torque but requiring specialist knowledge for EGR and turbo-related repairs.
items.descriptor: The 2.0L VW-sourced CRD diesel (BYL code) was the only diesel option for UK Avengers (2007-2010), offering strong torque but requiring specialist knowledge for EGR and turbo-related repairs.
items.familiesLabel: Common Dodge Avenger Diesel Engine Families
families: BYL - 2.0L CRD diesel (SE/SXT), EGR and turbo actuator concerns drive replacement demand
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and torque for towing
EGR valve clogging causing rough idle after 80k miles
Turbo actuator failure triggering limp mode on higher-mileage units
Timing chain tensioner wear documented on neglected examples
items.cta: Get quotes for Dodge Avenger Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Dodge Avenger Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Avengers dominated UK sales with 2.0L, 2.4L and V6 options; throttle body, ignition coil and cooling system failures are the most common replacement drivers across the range.
items.descriptor: Petrol Avengers dominated UK sales with 2.0L, 2.4L and V6 options; throttle body, ignition coil and cooling system failures are the most common replacement drivers across the range.
items.familiesLabel: Common Dodge Avenger Petrol Engine Families
families: ECN - 2.0L World Engine (entry SE/SXT), throttle body and coil failures common
EDG - 2.4L World Engine (volume seller), same failure patterns as ECN but more available
EER - 2.7L V6 (early SXT), oil sludge concerns limit long-term appeal
EGF - 3.5L V6 (R/T performance), cooling system vigilance essential
ERB - 3.6L Pentastar (late Heat/R-T), water pump and cam phaser issues noted
foundIn: Pre-facelift sedan (2007-2010) - ECN 2.0L, EDG 2.4L, EER 2.7L, EGF 3.5L
knownFor: Affordable running costs on 2.0L/2.4L World Engines
Throttle body failure causing stalling and ETC warning lights
Ignition coil degradation triggering misfires after 70k miles
Cooling system leaks (thermostat housing, water pump) on higher-mileage units

engineYears.h2: Dodge Avenger Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Dodge Avenger's UK production timeline (2007-2014), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Avenger launched in the UK with four-cylinder petrol and VW-sourced diesel options, establishing the core engine lineup for early SE and SXT trims.
keyChanges: UK market launch with SE and SXT trim levels
Introduction of 2.0L CRD diesel (BYL) alongside 2.0L/2.4L petrol World Engines
Optional 2.7L V6 for SXT and 3.5L V6 for R/T performance trim
knownFor: Strong initial UK interest in diesel BYL for motorway efficiency
Early reports of throttle body issues on ECN/EDG petrol units
R/T 3.5L V6 praised for performance but cooling system vigilance advised
Build quality concerns on interior plastics noted by UK reviewers
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2009 Dodge Avenger replacement engines
years.ticker: Dodge Avenger BYL engine replacement • Avenger EDG throttle body failure • Avenger ECN ignition coil cost

years.description: The 2010 model year saw the discontinuation of the 2.7L V6 and AWD option, simplifying the Avenger lineup to four-cylinder and 3.5L V6 petrol plus the BYL diesel.
keyChanges: 2.7L EER V6 discontinued across all trims
All-wheel drive option removed; Avenger now front-wheel drive only
Introduction of side and curtain airbags as standard equipment
knownFor: Simplified engine choices made parts sourcing easier for UK specialists
EDG 2.4L remained the volume seller with strong breaker network support
R/T 3.5L V6 values held well among enthusiast buyers
BYL diesel continued to attract high-mileage fleet buyers
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010 Dodge Avenger replacement engines

years.description: The 2011 refresh brought new styling, improved interior quality and the introduction of the 3.6L Pentastar V6, replacing the older 3.5L unit in R/T and new Heat trims.
keyChanges: Exterior refresh with new crosshair grille and LED taillights
Interior overhaul with soft-touch materials and updated infotainment
Introduction of 3.6L Pentastar V6 (ERB) replacing 3.5L EGF in performance trims
Trim restructuring: Express/Mainstreet/Lux/Heat replaced SE/SXT/R-T naming temporarily
knownFor: Pentastar ERB praised for refinement but water pump leaks noted after 80k miles
EDG 2.4L remained reliable with throttle body and coil issues still common
Improved interior quality addressed earlier UK owner complaints
Final UK Avengers (2013-2014) now sought by budget-conscious used buyers
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2014 Dodge Avenger replacement engines
years.ticker: Avenger ERB water pump replacement • Avenger EDG throttle body cost • Avenger Pentastar engine rebuild

faq.h2: Dodge Avenger Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Dodge Avenger engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Typical UK rebuilt engine prices for the Dodge Avenger range from £1,400 for a 2.0L petrol ECN to £4,800 for a 3.6L Pentastar ERB, supply only. Supply & fit adds £400-£900 depending on garage location and engine complexity.
keyPoints: BYL 2.0L diesel rebuilt: £1,800-£3,200 supply only
EDG 2.4L petrol rebuilt: £1,500-£2,800 supply only
ERB 3.6L Pentastar rebuilt: £3,000-£4,800 supply only
All prices include upgraded components addressing common failure points
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across EngineMarket network
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Dodge Avenger engine prices UK

items.question: What engine codes fit my Dodge Avenger?
items.answer: UK Dodge Avengers used BYL (2.0L diesel), ECN/EDG (2.0L/2.4L petrol World Engines), EER/EGF/ERB (2.7L/3.5L/3.6L V6 petrol). Enter your registration above to verify the exact code fitted to your vehicle.
keyPoints: BYL: 2.0L VW-sourced CRD diesel, 140hp, 2007-2010 SE/SXT
ECN: 2.0L World Engine petrol, 156hp, 2007-2009 SE/SXT
EDG: 2.4L World Engine petrol, 173hp, 2007-2014 most trims
ERB: 3.6L Pentastar V6, 283hp, 2011-2014 Heat/R-T
Engine code is stamped on block; DVLA lookup confirms fitment
items.cta: Get quotes for your specific Dodge Avenger engine code

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Dodge Avenger engine?
items.answer: For a well-maintained Avenger valued over £2,500, a rebuilt engine from £1,400 with 12-month warranty often makes financial sense versus part-exchange or scrap value.
keyPoints: Compare rebuilt engine cost against current Avenger market value
Factor in warranty coverage versus buying another used car
Supply & fit options remove installation hassle and risk
EngineMarket specialists can advise on value for your specific variant
Consider total cost of ownership including fuel, insurance and tax
items.cta: Calculate if engine replacement makes sense for your Avenger

items.question: How long does a reconditioned Dodge Avenger engine last?
items.answer: A properly reconditioned Dodge Avenger engine from a UK specialist should deliver 80,000-120,000 miles of reliable service with regular maintenance, backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
keyPoints: Reconditioning replaces worn gaskets, seals, bearings and consumables
Upgraded components address known failure points like throttle bodies
Follow manufacturer service intervals for oil, filters and coolant
Warranty covers manufacturing defects; maintenance remains owner responsibility
Documented service history maximises longevity and resale value
items.cta: View reconditioned Dodge Avenger engine options with warranty

items.question: Do I need to reprogram ECU after Avenger engine replacement?
items.answer: Most Dodge Avenger engine replacements require ECU adaptation reset or relearn procedure, which EngineMarket specialists include as standard with supply & fit packages.
keyPoints: Throttle body replacement always requires ECU adaptation reset
New engine may need idle relearn and fuel trim adaptation
Diagnostic tools required for BYL diesel turbo/EGR coding
Supply & fit packages include all necessary ECU procedures
Always confirm ECU coding is included in your quote
items.cta: Enquire about Dodge Avenger engine replacement with ECU coding

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Dodge Avenger?
items.answer: Engine swaps in the Dodge Avenger are complex due to ECU, wiring harness and emissions compliance requirements; we recommend sticking with the original engine code for MoT and insurance validity.
keyPoints: BYL diesel cannot be swapped for petrol without major ECU/wiring changes
V6 to four-cylinder swaps require transmission and ECU modifications
Non-original engine codes may fail MoT emissions testing
Insurance may be invalidated by unapproved engine modifications
EngineMarket specialists can advise on compatible like-for-like replacements
items.cta: Get advice on compatible Dodge Avenger engine replacements

items.question: What warranty comes with a Dodge Avenger replacement engine?
items.answer: Every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering manufacturing defects, with supply & fit packages extending coverage to labour where arranged.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt/reconditioned units
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Supply & fit packages may extend labour coverage through installing garage
Always confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare warranty terms for Dodge Avenger replacement engines

items.question: How do I know which Dodge Avenger engine I have?
items.answer: Your Dodge Avenger's engine code is stamped on the block near the cylinder head, visible on the VIN plate under the bonnet, or confirmed instantly by entering your registration number above.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block: BYL, ECN, EDG, EER, EGF or ERB
VIN plate under bonnet lists engine displacement and fuel type
V5C registration document shows engine capacity and fuel type
DVLA lookup via registration confirms exact variant and engine
EngineMarket specialists can verify compatibility before you order
items.cta: Identify your Dodge Avenger engine code instantly

items.question: Are used Dodge Avenger engines reliable?
items.answer: Used Dodge Avenger engines from reputable UK breakers can be reliable if compression-tested, with service history and no warning lights; always request a test report and warranty before purchase.
keyPoints: Request compression test results and mileage verification
Confirm no active fault codes or warning lights on donor vehicle
Prefer engines with documented service history and oil change records
Typical used engine warranty: 3-6 months parts only
Rebuilt/reconditioned options offer longer warranty for marginal price difference
items.cta: Check used Dodge Avenger engine availability with test reports

items.question: How long does Avenger engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only Dodge Avenger engines typically dispatch within 1-3 working days; supply & fit installations take 1-2 days at a vetted UK garage depending on variant and engine complexity.
keyPoints: Used engines often available for next-day dispatch from UK breakers
Rebuilt/reconditioned units may require 2-4 weeks lead time
Four-cylinder installations typically complete in one working day
V6/Pentastar installations may require 1.5-2 days due to complexity
EngineMarket coordinates delivery and garage booking for seamless service
